description:
A low-code API testing platform for development teams that care about creating test automation across any workflow.


ReadyAPI 3.67.0 includes the following updates, which we are excited to share with you. See Version History for changes in previous releases.
Integration Improvements
- Fixed Zephyr Essential test search in Jira Cloud projects: Resolved an issue where ReadyAPI did not display Test issues from Zephyr Essential-enabled Jira Cloud projects in the UI. Outdated API paths caused the issue and triggered the WARN [RestClient] Failed to get Zephyr tests warning message. ReadyAPI now uses the updated API endpoints that follow Atlassian’s migration guidelines (see the Atlassian developer changelog). This change restores correct test retrieval and display.
Performance Improvements
- Enhanced Performance module efficiency: This update increases transactions per second (TPS) in load tests. Current improvements deliver a 10–20% increase in throughput, depending on use case and test complexity.
  Planned enhancements for Q2 include upgrading to HTTP Client v5 and introducing Virtual Threads from Java 21 to further improve load testing throughput.
General Updates
- Library Updates: ReadyAPI 3.67.0 uses newer versions of several libraries compared to 3.66.0. If your scripts reference any classes from the libraries updated in ReadyAPI 3.67.0, review and update them as needed.
- Improved proxy handling in AI plugin HTTP client: The HTTP client in the SmartBear AI plugin now aligns with ReadyAPI proxy handling. This change ensures consistent behavior in environments that use proxy configurations.
  This update is delivered through the ReadyAPI SmartBear AI plugin. Reinstall the plugin to apply the update.
- UXM Plugin Update: Updated UXM plugin to version 1.3.3.
See Bug Fixes in ReadyAPI 3.67.0 for the complete list of fixes included in this release.
